CoInfoSim — Occupancy GMM → Real Monte Carlo Report

Channels: X₁ = TemperatureX₂ = HumidityX₃ = LightX₄ = CO₂X₅ = Humidity Ratio
Metric. Empirical test loss (misclassification rate on the fixed real evaluation split). The test-loss scale is preserved as-is. Channel subsets use compact variable notation (see sticky legend above); full sensor names appear in metadata and appendices.

1. Arm summary

Arm: GMM → Real

Class-conditional Gaussian mixture models are fitted to the standardized datatraining.txt pool. Balanced training samples are generated synthetically from these GMMs. Evaluation uses the fixed real Occupancy evaluation split (standardized datatest.txt + datatest2.txt).

2. Scientific role of this arm

This arm tests whether a more flexible multimodal class-conditional approximation better preserves real-data cooperative behavior than the single-Gaussian approximation. If GMM results closely match Real → Real, the multimodal synthetic distribution captures the key cooperative structure.

3. Dataset provenance summary

Dataset: UCI Occupancy Detection (ID 357, DOI 10.24432/C5X01N, Luis Candanedo, CC BY 4.0).
Training pool: datatraining.txt. Fixed real evaluation split: datatest.txt + datatest2.txt. Standardization parameters are estimated from the training pool only.

4. Run configuration

Arm
GMM → Real
Mode
full
Sample sizes (n_per_class)
[2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512]
Classifiers
Linear SVM, Logistic Regression, Gaussian Naive Bayes
Channel subsets evaluated
31 subsets
Fixed test set
fixed real Occupancy evaluation split (standardized datatest.txt + datatest2.txt) (12417 rows)
Min / max replications
100 / 2000
Replication batch size
20
Target CI95 half-width
0.01
Base seed
0
Runtime
1428.63 s
List all evaluated channel subsets

{X₁}, {X₂}, {X₃}, {X₄}, {X₅}, {X₁, X₂}, {X₁, X₃}, {X₁, X₄}, {X₁, X₅}, {X₂, X₃}, {X₂, X₄}, {X₂, X₅}, {X₃, X₄}, {X₃, X₅}, {X₄, X₅}, {X₁, X₂, X₃}, {X₁, X₂, X₄}, {X₁, X₂, X₅}, {X₁, X₃, X₄}, {X₁, X₃, X₅}, {X₁, X₄, X₅}, {X₂, X₃, X₄}, {X₂, X₃, X₅}, {X₂, X₄, X₅}, {X₃, X₄, X₅}, {X₁, X₂, X₃, X₄}, {X₁, X₂, X₃, X₅}, {X₁, X₂, X₄, X₅}, {X₁, X₃, X₄, X₅}, {X₂, X₃, X₄, X₅}, {X₁, X₂, X₃, X₄, X₅}

5. Training/evaluation protocol

Training source
Synthetic — class-conditional GMMs fitted to datatraining.txt
Evaluation source
Fixed real evaluation split: standardized datatest.txt + datatest2.txt
Model selection
BIC criterion over candidate component counts
Standardization
z-score, fitted on datatraining.txt only

6. Arm-specific data and model description

Estimated GMM training model

Use the selectors to inspect model-selection summary versus per-class component mean vectors and covariance matrices.

Model-selection configuration

Criterionbic
Covariance typefull
reg_covar1e-06
n_init5

7. Reproducibility controls

Model type
Class-conditional Gaussian Mixture Model per class
Fitting source
datatraining.txt (standardized)
Model selection criterion
BIC
Test split
datatest.txt + datatest2.txt (real, fixed)
Sampler type
SyntheticTrainRealTestSampler (GMMClassConditionalSampler)

8. Monte Carlo stopping rule

Standard-error stopping: replications continue in batches of 20 until the maximum 95% CI half-width (1.96 × SE) across all (subset, classifier) cells falls at or below the target (0.01) or the replication budget (2000) is exhausted.

n_per_classreplicationsstopping reasonmax CI half-width
22000max_budget0.0103
41320converged0.0100
8960converged0.0100
16680converged0.0100
32500converged0.0099
64260converged0.0100
128220converged0.0096
256120converged0.0091
512100converged0.0071

9. Monte Carlo precision diagnostics

Per n_per_class: the maximum standard error and the maximum 95% CI half-width (1.96 × SE) across all (subset, classifier) cells, compared against the convergence target (0.01, on the same CI95 half-width scale). All quantities preserve the empirical test-loss scale.

n_per_classReplicationsMax SEMax CI95 half-widthTarget CI95 half-widthStopping reason
220000.005230.010260.01000max_budget
413200.005080.009960.01000converged
89600.005090.009970.01000converged
166800.005100.010000.01000converged
325000.005060.009910.01000converged
642600.005090.009980.01000converged
1282200.004900.009600.01000converged
2561200.004630.009080.01000converged
5121000.003640.007130.01000converged

10. Loss curves

Loss curves are available in two complementary views. Best by cardinality shows the best 1-, 2-, 3-, and 4-channel subsets plus the full 5-channel reference. Nested cardinality shows all subsets of one selected cardinality against the same full 5-channel reference. Linear SVM is shown by default; error bars show ±1 SE.

11. Best subset comparison

Interpretable comparison of the best single channel, best pair, best triple, best four-channel subset and the full subset. The full-channel reference row is highlighted. Select a classifier below (Linear SVM default).

13. N-star diagnostics

For comparability, reference and competitor subsets are selected using the Real → Real arm at the largest evaluated n_per_class; the table and graph then show this report arm's own empirical test-loss curves. Select a classifier and reference-cardinality panel below. Dashed vertical markers show detected crossings when available.
